title: ophioglossum vulgatum var. typicum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum var. typicum, also known as the Adder's Tongue fern, is a species of vascular plant that belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and inflammation. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum var. typicum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower fever and improve respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the plant's fronds are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
